For instance, the gravitational acceleration near a black hole's event horizon can reach incredible magnitudes—approaching 10^17 meters per second squared—a scale that traditional computational algorithms can barely approach.

The quantum nature of space-time at these scales introduces non-linear dynamics that conventional computers struggle to capture.
